About: It is about a scene of prehistoric life based on evidence from fossil reconstructions.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Mary Anning.
It is associated with Henry De la Beche.
It is associated with Georg Scharf.
It is associated with William Conybeare.
It is associated with William Buckland.
It is associated with Georges Cuvier.
It is associated with Leopold von Buch.
It is associated with Georg August Goldfuss.
It is associated with John Phillips.
It is associated with Francois Jules Pictet de la Rive.
ConsistsOf: It consists of a watercolour painting.
It consists of a large version in oil on canvas.
It consists of a three-dimensional diorama.
CreatedBy: It was created by Henry De la Beche.
CreatedFor: It was created for Mary Anning's benefit.
CreatedTo: It was created to raise money for Anning's benefit.
It was created to assist Anning.
It was created to depict life in ancient Dorset.
HasA: It has a split level view showing action above and below the surface of the water.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by a split level view.
It is characterized by violent interaction between creatures.
It is characterized by the depiction of faeces that would fossilize into coprolites.
Influenced: It influenced several other depictions of prehistoric life.
It influenced Georg August Goldfuss's drawing of a Jurassic scene.
It influenced John Phillips's woodcut of a prehistoric scene.
It influenced an illustration in a French illustrated dictionary of natural history.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by William Buckland's description of the Lias' food chain.
It was influenced by Conybeare's verbal description of marine reptiles.
It was influenced by Georges Cuvier's drawings of prehistoric creatures.
It was influenced by Conybeare's cartoon of Buckland.
IsA: It is the first pictorial representation of a scene of prehistoric life based on evidence from fossil reconstructions.
It is a genre now known as paleoart.
It is the first depiction of a scene from deep time to see even limited publication.
It is the first known example of an aquarium view.
It is a large version in oil on canvas.
KnownAs: It is known as a more ancient Dorset.
KnownFor: It is known for being the first pictorial representation of a scene of prehistoric life based on evidence from fossil reconstructions.
It is known for its split level view.
It is known for influencing later depictions of prehistoric life.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Lyme Regis, Dorset.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Georg Scharf.
PublishedAs: It was published as lithographic prints.
PublishedIn: It was published in a book.
It was published in the Penny Magazine.
It was published in a French illustrated dictionary of natural history.
It was published in Elementary Treatise on Paleontology.
UsedAs: It is used as a prop in geology lectures.
UsedFor: It is used for educational purposes.
It is used for raising money for Anning's benefit.
It is used for educational purposes.
It is used for illustrating advances in palaeontology.
UsedIn: It is used in scientific circles.
It is used in scientific and popular literature.
It is used in geology lectures.
It is used in a book.
UsedTo: It is used to understand the deep past.
